skill.name: ask-graphql-mcp
skill.description: Handle Web3 and on-chain questions through GraphQL endpoints using Ask GraphQL MCP. Supports SubQuery/SubGraph endpoints for blockchain data queries, protocol analysis, and token metrics.
skill.summary: Query blockchain data via GraphQL endpoints using Ask GraphQL MCP for Web3 analysis and on-chain metrics.
skill.author: SN-Hermes

security_audit.summary: All 146 static analyzer findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The skill is a legitimate Web3 GraphQL query tool that connects to user-provided endpoints via the Ask GraphQL MCP gateway. Network URLs are documented service endpoints. Markdown backticks in documentation were incorrectly flagged as shell execution. API key references are template placeholders for user configuration.

content.value_statement: Ask complex questions about blockchain data without writing GraphQL queries manually. This skill connects to SubQuery and SubGraph endpoints to retrieve on-chain metrics, protocol data, and token information through natural language.
actual_capabilities: Execute natural language queries against SubQuery and SubGraph GraphQL endpoints
Discover GraphQL endpoints for blockchain projects automatically
Analyze token metrics, staking data, and governance information
Debug and optimize GraphQL queries for on-chain data
Support both free-tier and paid API access modes
Return structured analysis with assumptions and query explanations
limitations: Requires a valid GraphQL endpoint URL for the target blockchain project
Free tier has rate limits that require API key upgrade for heavy usage
Cannot access private or authenticated endpoints without proper credentials
Query complexity may cause timeouts for very large datasets

use_cases.title: DeFi Protocol Analysis
use_cases.description: Query liquidity pools, trading volumes, and user activity metrics from DeFi protocols indexed by SubQuery.
use_cases.target_user: DeFi analysts and researchers tracking protocol performance

use_cases.title: Token Holder Insights
use_cases.description: Analyze token distribution, holder counts, transfer patterns, and staking statistics for any indexed token.
use_cases.target_user: Crypto investors and portfolio managers researching token economics

use_cases.title: Smart Contract Debugging
use_cases.description: Validate GraphQL queries, check field availability, and optimize query performance for indexed contract data.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building dApps that consume indexed blockchain data

prompt_templates.title: Basic Endpoint Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the GraphQL endpoint at [URL] and tell me what data domains are available for this project.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time exploration of a new blockchain project's indexed data

prompt_templates.title: Token Metrics Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the endpoint [URL], find the top 10 token holders by balance and show their holdings with percentages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyzing token distribution and whale concentrations

prompt_templates.title: Transaction Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the last 100 transactions on this endpoint and summarize the most common transaction types and their frequencies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding protocol activity patterns and user behavior

prompt_templates.title: Custom Query Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: This GraphQL query is failing with error [ERROR]. Analyze the schema at [URL] and rewrite a working query that achieves the same goal.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fixing broken queries and learning correct field names and structure

output_examples.input: Analyze the top liquidity pools by TVL on this SubQuery endpoint
output: Assumptions: Using the provided SubQuery endpoint for a DeFi protocol
Summary: Retrieved 15 liquidity pools sorted by Total Value Locked
Top Pool: ETH-USDC with $12.5M TVL, 2.3% APY, 1,247 active LPs
Query used: pools(orderBy: totalValueLockedUSD, orderDirection: desc, first: 15)
Next step: Request specific pool details or historical TVL trends

output_examples.input: Show me the governance proposal activity for this DAO
output: Assumptions: Querying governance module from the indexed DAO endpoint
Summary: Found 47 proposals total, 12 active, 35 executed
Active Proposals: 3 voting now, 2 in execution delay
Voter Turnout: Average 18.5% participation across all proposals
Next step: Query specific proposal details or voter analytics

best_practices: Always provide the GraphQL endpoint URL when asking blockchain questions for fastest results
Start with free tier and upgrade to paid API key only when rate limits are reached
Use specific, focused questions rather than broad requests for more accurate query generation
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to bypass the MCP gateway with direct GraphQL calls unless explicitly requested
Avoid sending queries without an endpoint URL, which triggers unnecessary discovery steps
Do not ignore rate limit signals, as switching to paid API key is faster than waiting for reset

faq.question: What GraphQL endpoints does this skill support?
faq.answer: Any SubQuery or SubGraph endpoint that follows standard GraphQL schema conventions. Provide the endpoint URL when asking questions for best results.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, the free tier works without an API key. You only need an API key if you hit free-tier rate limits or want higher quota access.

faq.question: How do I get an API key if I need one?
faq.answer: Visit https://ask.hermes-subnet.ai/billing/api-keys/ to create your API key. Then provide it to switch to paid mode with higher limits.

faq.question: What happens if my query times out?
faq.answer: Complex queries have a 2-minute timeout. If exceeded, try breaking your question into smaller parts or request a more optimized query.

faq.question: Can this skill query private or authenticated endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes, provide the X-ENDPOINT-AUTHORIZATION header value when your endpoint requires authentication. The skill will include it in requests.

faq.question: What blockchains are supported?
faq.answer: Any blockchain with a SubQuery or SubGraph indexer endpoint. Popular networks include Ethereum, Polygon, BSC, Avalanche, and many others.
